differential_diagnoses: Kaposi's varicelliform eruption (eczema herpeticum) with underlying erythroderma
Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS) syndrome
Paraneoplastic erythroderma secondary to lung malignancy
Infectious erythroderma (e.g., Staphylococcal scalded skin syndrome or generalized bacterial infection)
next_steps: Perform repeat skin biopsy with immunohistochemistry (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD7, CD30, CD20) and T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies
Perform flow cytometry on peripheral blood to assess for Sézary cells
Obtain PET/CT scan to guide biopsy of the lung mass and axillary lymph nodes
Perform excisional biopsy of the most accessible suspicious lymph node
Consult Hematology-Oncology and Dermatology specialists for multidisciplinary review
Continue treatment for HSV and secondary bacterial superinfection while awaiting definitive pathology
